turns.content: Why does my closure even compile when I try to pass a struct with a Vec inside it into thread::spawn? It feels like the borrow checker is just giving up on me.

It's not giving up; it's enforcing safety. When you move a closure into thread::spawn, the compiler needs to ensure that the data inside can be safely sent across threads and lives forever. That means every type in your struct must implement Send and have 'static lifetime.
But I thought I could just move ownership? Why does it still complain about the borrow checker even after I use move?

The move keyword only transfers ownership, but thread::spawn has stricter requirements. It needs to guarantee that nothing inside your closure can be borrowed or modified by another thread at the same time. So if you have a Vec of owned data, that's fine because it implements Send and 'static.
What about references? I keep trying to put &mut T in my closure but it fails immediately. Is there any way to make mutable access across threads?

Not really, and that's by design. A mutable reference like &mut T doesn't satisfy 'static because the reference itself might expire. You can only share immutable references or owned data safely in this context.
So I should just avoid using mutable state entirely? That feels restrictive for a real application where I need to update counters.

You don't have to give up on mutable state. Instead of putting a reference or a struct with references inside the closure, you can pass owned data that implements Send and 'static. For example, use an AtomicU32 wrapped in your struct so each thread gets its own copy via clone or similar.
Ah, so I can just create a new instance of my struct for each spawned thread? That sounds like what I was trying to do but got confused by the error messages.

Exactly. Instead of sharing mutable state through references which block Send, you clone or move owned data into separate instances per thread. Then each closure can safely mutate its own local copy without worrying about races from other threads.
